entry.title: Dynamic Text in Measure - Analytics Cloud - Live Data Model
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a dashboard with a Live Data Model from a SAP BW Query. In the story, I have a restricted Key Figure with an Input Date Control on Story level. In this way, I have components that show measures, filtered by CM-current month, YTM-year to month, LYTM-last year to month, YOY-year over year and so on.

Is there a way to show the actual range in the name of the measure as Dynamic Text?

It can get a bit confusing as to what is being shown in a component.

An example of an input control restricting the measure:

For greater clarity in the chart, I would like the actual time range in the name of the measure.

Or if at least I can use scripting to change the text in the Subtitle?

Also, is there a way to limit it to DECEMBER of PREVIOUS YEAR of the selected input control?

entry.title: Re: Project cost vs payroll posting in professional servi...
entry.content:
Hello @LeventePosza,

the recommendation for professional service customers is to use the cost rates for services (usually maintained per year) and time recordings to determine the credit of the cost center and the debit of the workpackage. Over- or underconsumption based on difference between credit towards projects and debit via payroll and other cost postings on the cost center remain there.

Regarding your questions:

1. Payroll postings shouldn't be linked to the WBS as the cost on the WBS are already posted based on hours and cost rates for service

2. It is not standard practice to repost payroll costs to WBS

3. Under/Over-realization remains on the cost center

4. Best practice for revenue in time and expense contracts is to maintain a project specific sales price (condition PCP0); for fix price the standard revenue recognition key SPFC is based on a cost based POC taken into consideration plan cost and actual cost based on time recordings and maintained cost rates per hour.

Best regards,

David

entry.title: Re: invoice number range
entry.content:
Hi 

The only feasible option in this scenario is to define separate document types according to plant(s)  and assign distinct number ranges accordingly.

  • For example:
    Plant 0001 → Document Type ZIV1
    Plant 0002 → Document Type ZIV2  …and so on.

You can then assign different number ranges to each document type as per plant-specific requirements.
Additionally, it's recommended to maintain user authorizations based on document types and/or plants to ensure appropriate control and access.

Thanks 
Santosh

entry.title: Re: Doesn't seem like for Learning I can use Item Connect...
entry.content:
Sf Learning explicitly sets OCN imported items as External as to indicate they will receive OCN API updates/completions and should not be updated from this by an item connector, see KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2481855 
You can go to the Libraries and then add OCN items via search for items with the filter for this external-course classification to add accordingly.

entry.title: Re: BoM using 3rd party during manufacturing process
entry.content:
Hi Rossco,

You need to create three levels of production version for this process. 

1. Raw steel frames (Inhouse production - BOM/Routing/PV) 

2. Galvanized product (subcontracting - BOM/Purchase info record with Price for service and PV)

3. Finished steel (Inhouse production - BOM/Routing/PV)

Product costing will capture the manufacturing and service cost when you costing run for Finished product. 

Thanks

Karthik

entry.title: SAP TM Condition for Calculation Resolution Base STAGE
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently struggled with a condition I’m trying to use for charge calculation, and I’m wondering if anyone can help me.

Let me explain my situation.

 

I’m using Calculation Resolution Base = STAGE.

Some carriers calculates their charges based on weight up to a specific threshold, and beyond this balance point, the charge is based on the number of pallets.

 

I’m having trouble defining a condition that reads this specific threshold correctly.

Indeed, the only way I have found to use weight in the condition is by using the ROOT weight. But is it not the weight of the stage, it is the total weight of the truck. So, the calculation is not accurate.

 

Example:

For customers A and B, I have the following charge calculation rules:

  • For weights less than or equal to 1000 kg : 50€ per 100 kg.
  • For weight over than 1000 kg : 100€ per pallet.

Customer A bought 600 kg of goods (which equals to 2 pallets)

Customer B bought 1200 kg of goods (which equal to 4 pallets)

I expect the following charge calculation:

  • Customer A = 600kgs of goods. It is lower than 1000kgs so, 50€/100kgs so, 50*6=300€
  • Customer B = 1200kgs of goods. It is upper than 1000kgs so, 100€/pallet so, 100*4=400€

 

But, since the condition is using the ROOT weight, which is 600+1200=1800 (above 1000 kg), the calculation is:

  • Customer A = 2 pallets = 200€
  • Customer B = 4 pallets = 400€

So my question is: with calculation resolution base = STAGE, is it possible to access the stage weight in the PRECON condition ?I have tried using a data crawler but I’m not able to get the expected behavior..

 

Thanks for your help,

entry.title: Re: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzon...
entry.content:
Maybe I should generalize my question a bit more.

We use the SAP Workzone to supply generalized, Internet based, access to Fiori applications of several SAP landscapes. If such a Fiori app contains references to external data sources (such as SAP content repository) then the app must be modified to work in 2 situations:

1. when the user is connected to the company on-premise network only
2. when the user is connected to SAP Workzone only.

In situation 1 we have no problems. The user is able to reach the content repository which is located on-premise. 
In situation 2, the SAP workzone browser tries to reach the content repository, but can only do that when:

a) it is exposed via SCC and the calling application uses the SCC destination OR
b) when the client-side (browser) call in the Fiori is replaced by a server-side call (in which case the SAP backend does the call to the content server).

entry.title: Re: Repeat on function in ME21N
entry.content:
Mystery solve  😎 and this principle applies the same to the delivery address "Repeat ON"  

1. You need to complete all required fields on the Account Assignments tab (G/L Account, Cost Center, etc).  Let's say you create this on you item 1.

2. Select the field "Repeat On" (only once)

3. Continue to create your item 2, 3, etc. The system will repeat  the account Assignant from Line 1 as expected.

You cannot go back to a previously completed line or section and then try to apply the Repeat function.

For example:

  • If you complete Line 1 and Line 2, then go back to Line 1 and click Repeat — it won’t work.
  • The Repeat function must be used immediately after completing the section you want to repeat.

entry.title: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG with Field names
entry.content:
Hi!

I have a program I have to modify. The program uses FM 'FREE_SELECTIONS_INIT' and FM 'FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG' to display the fields of the previously given SAP table (eg. ERDK), to add select options for filtering data.

I have to add field names near the field labels as shown on the image below, but I cant figure out how.
Can you help me please?

 

In this case:
GT_ROWTAB-PRIM_TAB = 'ERDK'
GT_FREE is initial
GV_SELID = 'DS000001'

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_free_selection,
        expr    TYPE rsds_texpr,
        where   TYPE rsds_twhere,
        fields  TYPE rsdsfields_t,
        field_r TYPE rsds_trange,
       END OF   ty_free_selection.

DATA: gt_rowtab TYPE rsdstabs_t.
DATA: gt_free   TYPE ty_free_selection. 
DATA: gv_selid  TYPE dynselid.

  CALL FUNCTION 'FREE_SELECTIONS_INIT'
    EXPORTING
      kind                  = 'T'
      expressions           = gt_free-expr
    IMPORTING
      selection_id          = gv_selid
      where_clauses         = gt_free-where
      expressions           = gt_free-expr
      field_ranges          = gt_free-field_r
    TABLES
      tables_tab            = gt_rowtab
      fields_tab            = gt_free-fields.

  CALL FUNCTION 'FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG'
    EXPORTING
      selection_id  = gv_selid
      as_window     = 'X'
      start_row     = 3
      start_col     = 8
    IMPORTING
      where_clauses = gt_free-where
      expressions   = gt_free-expr
      field_ranges  = gt_free-field_r
    TABLES
      fields_tab    = gt_free-fields.

entry.title: Re: How to edit a process during approval workflow withou...
entry.content:
Hi, 

You guessed correctly that an edit to the Process Model means a new revision of the process model and the approval is linked to the revision, not to the model ID. And it is meaningful that way. If one could make changes after the approval without affecting the approval, that would not be compliant. 

Hope this helps. 

Best,

Adriana

entry.title: Inconsistencies Between Quality and Production Environments in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We’re encountering critical inconsistencies between the Quality and Production environments in our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system, particularly affecting payment processes. These discrepancies are impacting our client’s daily operations, and we would appreciate your insights:

  1. Payment Advice Output Issue:
    Despite correctly configuring the Output Parameter Determination (OPD) for payment advice forms, the Automatic Payment Program in Production continues to trigger and send an outdated form that is not included in the OPD settings. The new form, which is correctly configured and active in OPD, is being ignored. This issue does not occur in the Quality environment.

  2. Payment File Format Discrepancy:
    In CBC, each payment method is assigned a specific Payment Medium Format under “Set Up Payment Methods for Each Country/Region.” However, in Production, the Manage Payment Media app generates payment files using a single, incorrect format across all methods, ignoring the CBC configuration.

Has anyone experienced similar issues or can offer guidance on:

  • Why OPD settings might be bypassed in Production?
  • How to ensure CBC payment method configurations are correctly reflected in Production?

Any help in diagnosing and resolving these inconsistencies would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SF LMS Event Based Integration
entry.content:
Hi @MelanieBelzile 

here are my replies: 
Can you clarify if the 'total hours' is coming separate to the completion event that is called in Intelligent Services? => yes, comes separately and is not part of completion event.

Do you then grab total hours from a separate API to gather additional data together for your external system? => yes from SF LMS API

As well, what is your approx. volume of users in LMS? As you mention this is 'almost real time', I am curious how many events are called on a consistent basis for your organization. => in average 800 events per week day

Lastly, can you confirm that the 'Event Connector' in IS, is calling only NEW completion events/records? Or is it calling all learning history in your system?

=> in SF BizX IS, it is the event Connector. Via SF LMS API we retrieve the employee’s last learning history record (not all).

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Text in Measure - Analytics Cloud - Live Data...
entry.content:
hi jbreznar,
you could use BW date variables. A step_1 variable value could be predefined with some logic and/or end user adjust step_1 variable in your SAC story as they like.  BW then derives several step_2 (CM, YTM,...)  variables which will be used to limit the date range in the keyfigures and by using replacement path variables you can also use them for naming your keyfigures.
AFAIK there is no other way to show defined date ranges in your keyfigures.
BR
Edgar

entry.content:
Without setting up a test app your looping rule looks ok assuming your CreateRelatedMedia properly defines the ParentLink and optionally the Properties.  One thing you could try is to make the media an array with just the one object.  Perhaps the client always expecting an array and the error is saying since you sent in a single object it can't loop over it. 

I will have to try and setup a test to replicate it sometime next week when I have some free time.

entry.title: Re: Moving Q-stock between Plants
entry.content:
I think this is an unique business case. You can also try post to unrestricted at plant A and perform 08 inspection at plant B when you do STO/Shipping. 

You may have to elaborate why you need to perform inspection at plant A and release it in plant B unless there are restrictions in product flow between these plants. 

 

Cheers 

Karthik

entry.title: Re: MDK style is not applying to iOS app
entry.content: You should review your Styles.less.  One thing I have seen is if you have an invalid style reference higher up in the Styles.less it will cause the styles after that to not be loaded and therefore not apply.

entry.title: Re: Custom MDK code for barcode reading logic is not work...
entry.content:
Unless the camera is blocked by the cable (it is on my zebra device) you should be able to debug on the physical device while connected to your computer.

If it is blocked then you will need to add additional logging and then grab the client log to see what it occurring.

entry.title: Re: Best Approach to Trigger SAP CPI Interface Periodical...
entry.content: Have you considered calling a odata endpoint from SAP BTP without any parameters and doing all the data selection and filtration in SAP Interface code in S4 using constant tables , In such cases you would not be changing any BTP CI job parameters , all can be controlled using a sm30 for tvarvc ( or custom constant tables) , BTP CI job can be scheduled in BTP and input parameters would be managed in S4 side

entry.title: You are the Voice of HR! Participate Now in Sapient Insights Group’s 28th Annual HR Systems Survey.
entry.content:
The 28th Annual HR Systems Survey, conducted by Sapient Insights Group, is now open for participation through June 25th, 2025.  Your voice is the future of HR Technology!

 

TAKE THE SURVEY

This survey is valuable for anyone supporting their organization's HR systems and practices, interested in sharing their vendor experiences, and giving back to the HR community.

All responses are confidential and only used in aggregate results.

As a Thank You for your participation, you will receive the following:

  • Continuing education - 2 hour credit toward IHRIM’s HRIP recertification
  • A personalized data snapshot filtered by Industry or Region – your pick
  • A full copy of the Sapient Insights 2025-2026 HR Systems Survey Report, 28th Annual Edition — an $1800 value [to be released in Fall 2025]

 

The first 100 respondents who complete the minimum survey requirements will also receive a $10 gift card of their choice or option to make a charitable donation, and our 28th, 128th, 528th, 1028th, and 2028th respondents will be awarded a $100 gift card of their choice or option to make a charitable donation.

 

The 28th Edition of the Annual HR Systems Survey includes these exciting updates:

 

  • Extensive questions on the use of AI / Machine Learning throughout the survey
  • More options to collaborate with colleagues to complete the survey
  • A dynamic table of contents that allows you to easily navigate through the survey, making the best use of your time and expertise
  • Easily store your previous answers, so there is no pressure to complete the survey in one sitting
  • A 24 hour response time to any technical issues or survey questions

 

Survey topics include:

  • HR strategy, process, and structure
  • HR technology adoption in all applications
  • HR practices in Benefits, Talent Management, and Workforce planning
  • HRIS governance & organization structure
  • Functional & HR Tech support services
  • Deployment roadmaps, resourcing, and budgeting
  • Implementation, maintenance, and integration of HR Tech platforms
  • Change management and adaptive leadership practices
  • Emerging and innovative technology and trends

*The survey takes approximately 15-60+ minutes of your time and may be forwarded to other team members for additional feedback and knowledge sharing.

If you would like to preview the survey or if you have any additional questions before taking the survey, please contact Tammy Smith with the Sapient Insights Research team at research@sapientinsights.com.         

The survey results will debut at the HR Technology Conference September 16-18th 2025. Our team looks forward to seeing you there! If you would like to purchase a copy of our 2024-2025 Annual HR Systems Report, you can do so here.

Thank you for sharing your experience with SAP SuccessFactors!

entry.title: CPQ Release changes to UI?
entry.content:
Hello Team,

SAP make changes to the CPQ UI in releases, especially quarterly releases. Can clearer up-front communication be provided to customers when such changes are made, please? 

If inadequate input or warning is provided to the customer then the end user is significantly impacted. 

Questions:

  1. What is the criteria for making UI changes?
  2. How is it decided upon considering customers?
  3. Does SAP consult customers, i.e. end users of CPQ?
  4. Is it possible in future to align more readily with CPQ customers and provide advanced warning to UI changes?

Thanks and regards,

Marc

entry.title: Re: Automating Provisioning Jobs and Job Logs using SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS) API
entry.content:
Trying to automate daily health check report for your SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS) jobs.

The report should:

  • Be generated for a specific date (e.g. today or a user-specified date)

  • Include all jobs (regardless of status) that ran for a list of known system names

  • Extract and display:

    •  

    • name

    • Job status

    • Start & End times

    • Trigger type

    • Job type

    • Duration

    • Error messages (if any)

  • Be presented in an HTML file with a clean, readable layout

Limitation Description

Missing API supportThe critical endpoints that would let you dynamically query job activity are not available in your SAP IPS tenant
 /jobHistoriesReturns 404 — would’ve allowed fetching job runs per day
/jobExecutionsReturns 404 — would’ve allowed filtering by date/system name
 /jobDefinitionsReturns 404 — would’ve allowed mapping system names to job definitions
Only /jobLogs/{jobId} worksBut it requires you to manually provide job IDs, which change daily
No endpoint to discover jobId by system/dateWhich prevents fully automated discovery of job runs

entry.title: Colgate's Journey with SAP BTP: Preserving HR Admin Functionality from SAP HCM to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Webinar Event Date and Time:   17 July 2025 -   Time: 11:00 EST / 17:00 CET

The Challenge:
When transitioning from on-premises SAP HCM to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, HRIT leaders face a crucial question: How do you preserve the custom-built functionality your business relies on while embracing cloud benefits?

The Solution:
Please Join SAP customer, Colgate, in this SAP partner-led event with Comentec, as they share their practical journey of leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to not only maintain but also enhance critical HR administrative capabilities during their cloud migration, resulting in reduced implementation time and improved user satisfaction.

In This Webinar, You'll Discover:

  • Practical Migration Strategies: How Colgate identified, prioritized, and successfully transitioned their essential custom HR functionality to the cloud
  • Skills Transition Framework: How Colgate's ABAP developers adapted their existing skills to cloud development, accelerating adoption while reducing training costs
  • Design Principles: How implementing simple, reusable, and sustainable components created a future-proof HR landscape that's easier to maintain
  • Real-World Examples: See how Colgate preserved vital admin capabilities including:
    • Cloud-based payroll interface management (equivalent to PU12 in SAP HCM)
    • Organizational structure-based selection tools for custom processes

Key Takeaways:

  • Skill Reapplication: Discover how your on-premise ABAP resources can effectively transition to cloud development using SAP BTP
  • Enhanced User Experience: Learn how SAP BTP's Fiori Elements framework offers more robust solutions than your current on-premises environment
  • Strategic Advantage: Understand how designing with SAP standards while building reusable components reduces development effort, accelerates implementation, and simplifies support

Who Should Attend:
● HRIT leaders owning the strategy for SAP SuccessFactors migration
● HR Technology teams concerned about preserving critical custom functionality
● SAP HCM developers looking to transfer their skills to the cloud environment
● Business stakeholders wanting to ensure seamless HR operations during migration

This session builds upon our previous webinars with PepsiCo and on EC Integration, taking your SAP BTP knowledge to the next level with practical implementation insights.

Register now to learn how SAP BTP can turn your cloud migration into an opportunity to create an even better HR technology experience.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Can an automation be ran without the SAP Desktop Agent?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working an SAP Build Process Automation pilot project and have reached a point where the team is ready to start looking at deployment options in terms of how the automation will be ran. It seems like the desktop agent is necessary to run a scheduled unattended trigger. Ultimately, we want to avoid having to setup a virtual machine to carry out this particular automation. The below image contains our automation process and the options we see available. What would be the best way to run this particular scenario without the Desktop Agent? The automation currently uses the Outlook SDK, but research has led us to find that if we use Microsoft 365 SDK instead, we can run this scenario without the agent. Are these findings valid? If not, I would appreciate feedback on the best approach to help us move forward!

 

 

Regards,

Patrick Pienkowski

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Stock Room Management
entry.content: @Julien_M , Item category determination occurs in the delivery and is independent of whether you use EWM, Stock Room Management, or even no warehouse solution.  You do not need EWM for it.  There is a separate item *type* determination in the EWM ODO which drives some of the EWM processes *if* you are using EWM.  You would, of course, need EWM for that and not Stock Room Management.

entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm using mail sender adapter to read email from inbox, and I set mail attribute to enable Mail package
Encoding format either I chose 'none' or  'base64'

however PI receives special characters inside  of XML after read the email
Example of wrong data format in 'from'  field or  'To'  field or 'Message_ID' field ( where we can special  characters <xxxx>) 
Due to this including special characters format, mapping transformation throws an error.

Is their any special module configuration in Mail sender which avoid special characters while reading the email from Inbox

Kindly please suggest your valuable inputs.

Thank you

Sateesh

entry.title: Settlement management for paying commissions
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have the following commissions process today.

  1. Sales rep participates as a partner on sales orders
  2. Commission is calculated using a condition type in pricing procedure
  3.  Upon invoicing the sale, commission is transferred to Vistex using an incentive document and accrued
  4. Every two weeks the accrued documents are settled using a settlement calendar and commission aggregated and sent to an external payroll system.

I believe settlement management should be able to handle the above business scenario. We are planning to move to S4 RISE PCE and looking to simplify our landscape.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: CALL TRANSFORMATION - CX_ST_MATCH_ELEMENT exception

entry.title: SFAPI Data Dictionary
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec 

As we all know that SFAPI data dictionary is deprecated, what is the alternate solution to get the entity for compensation? In the ODATA API data dictionary, I do not see the entity which has all the standard and custom fields related to the compensation plan template. Therefore, could you please tell me about the alternative solution for the SFAPI data dictionary in SF.

Appreciate your quick response.

Thank you!

entry.title: Verification field on RF screen is not editable
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to enable and scan the product field rather than manually entering it on the RF screen When clicking on F2NewItm in the PI count process—Counting by Bin.

There is an existing code in FM which is disabling the MATNR-VERIF field. I have commented that code and now it is enabled.  However, it is still not editable. I have checked verification profile config and it is correct.

Above yellow highlighted field is /SCWM/S_RF_INVENTORY_MAT-MATNR_VERIF

Any idea on why the verification field is not editable ? 

Thank you.

Regards,

Karthik

EWM - Radio Frequency

entry.title: Add Field Reception Point (EMPST) at Item Level in Standard Application Template ID F4293
entry.content: We are currently wanted to use the standard SAP Fiori application with Template ID F4293 and require the inclusion of the field Reception Point (EMPST) at the item level. This field is essential for our business. We would like to request guidance or documentation on how this field can be integrated into the standard application template, or if there is any recommended enhancement approach provided by SAP to achieve this requirement.

entry.title: How to obtain License Key to S/4 HANA On-Premise Systems?
entry.content:
Hello,

How can I obtain a License Key to SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise Systems?

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to obtain License Key to S/4 HANA On-Premise Syst...
entry.content:
Hello,

To generate licenses for On-Premise systems, this action must be performed by the customer. 
In the case of generating licenses for the SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise System, as outlined in KBA 2366280 - How to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA (all versions) - SAP for Me, it is necessary to access SAP for Me > License Keys and request the license by providing the required data, such as Product, System Version, System ID, System Name, System Type, Database, and Operating System. Additionally, you will need to select the license type and provide the hardware key.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Developer News June 5th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/06/05/sap-developer-news-june-5th-2025/ 

New SAP Certified Associate - SAP Business Data Cloud 

  • Webinar Series: Architecting Your SAP Business Data Cloud Journey – From Data to Intelligence: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-blog-posts/new-webinar-series-architecting-your-sap-business-data-cloud-journey-from/ba-p/14104348  
  • New SAP Certified Associate - SAP Business Data Cloud: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-sap-business-data-cloud  
  • Learning Journey: Exploring SAP Business Data Cloud: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-sap-business-data-cloud  

New ways to install the Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP 

  • Installation information https://sap.github.io/terraform-exporter-btp/install/ 

SAP CodeJam Roadshow – US AI 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-new-york-new-york-usa/ev-p/14099369 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-palo-alto-ca-usa/ev-p/14101603 
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-houston-tx-usa/ev-p/14101612 

SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2025 – Latin America edition 

  • SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2025 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷 🇵🇪 🇲🇽 🇨🇴 (July 25th - Aug 12th 2024): https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2025-latin-america-edition-july-25th-aug-12th-2024/ba-p/14093930  

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 New SAP Certified Associate - SAP Business Data Cloud 

1:08 New ways to install the Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP 

2:01 SAP CodeJam Roadshow – US AI 

3:33 SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2025 - Latin America edition 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for June the 5th, 2025.

[Witalij] This month, we at SAP launched a new webinar series titled Architecting Your SAP Business Data Cloud Journey, From Data to Intelligence. This series kicks off this week with a discussion on data and AI strategy across SAP business applications, followed by a deep dive into our latest data offering, SAP Business Data Cloud. Additional topics include BWM modernization, SAP Databricks, and transition paths to SAP BBC. Building on this foundation, we invite you to validate your expertise by taking the new SAP Business Data Cloud certification exam. The certification confirms your solid understanding of SAP Business Data Cloud, and it demonstrates that you possess both the broad overview and essential skills needed to contribute effectively within project teams. enjoy the learning journey and good luck with the exam links are in the description

[DJ] there's a great update for the terraform exporter for sap btp which we've covered in previous episodes on this show so as well now as being able to download the binary for your os the binary of the tool you can now install the tool via package managers for Mac OS there's brew for windows there's winget and soon also chocolatey and of course for linux you have installation package manager mechanisms for debian based and rpm based distributions great this is going to make the management of the life cycle of the tool a lot more comfortable for us check out the updated installation information link is in the description

[Rich] Hello, friends. Rich Heilman here. Hope everyone is doing well. I just wanted to pop on here and let you all know about the SAP Code Jam Roadshow in the U.S. that is happening in just about two weeks. This roadshow will include three stops, and we will be focusing on the topic of getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. The first stop will be on June 23rd in the very heart of New York City. I always love doing these short trips to New York City. It sort of fulfills my childhood craving to live and work in the big city, even for just a day or two. So I'm looking forward to that. And then Tom and I will attempt to jump on a plane out of Newark, New Jersey. wish us luck and fly to the west coast for our next stop on June 25th in beautiful palo alto California if you haven't visited the bay area absolutely stunning sights especially for us East coasters so check it out and then from there I'm going to attempt to get back to Newark wish me luck and Tom will then be joined by Mimiki for the last stop in Houston on June 27th So it's shaping up to be a great trip, but that's not the only roadshows happening this year. And I know that my friend Antonio has some details on that. So over to you, Antonio.

[Antonio]Thanks, Rich. Hola, SAP developers. We just announced the second Latin America Code Jam Roadshow, which will take place between the 25th of July and the 12th of August. This time, Rich and Tom will join the roadshow, and we will be doing CodeJams on ABAP Cloud and the Generative AI Hub. We kick things off with the Generative AI CodeJam in Sao Paulo, Brazil. And then we'll be doing both CodeJams in Lima, Peru, Mexico City, and Bogota, Colombia. Similar to last year, we will also do meetups in the different locations. Vamos a ter un encontro SAPI en Sao Paulo. En quedadas en Lima,Ciudad de México y Bogotá. Make sure to check out the blog post to find all the details of the roadshow and check out each event to learn which developer advocate will be at the different events. Até pronto. Nos vemos pronto.

entry.title: Re: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content:
Hi @Cocquerel,
Thanks for your questions.  No, Joule does not require MCP for interacting with custom Joule Skills. Given the nature of Skills, MCP is not necessary at this stage. However, for future Joule Agents, MCP will be leveraged when the use case calls for it.

MCP is still a relatively new protocol, and we are currently evaluating its applicability across different scenarios. We are actively exploring how a custom MCP server could be integrated as a tool within Joule Studio to support more advanced use cases.
